page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is consistently low, with stress-ng, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%.

From a workload perspective, the ecs.n4.8xlarge is suited for multi-threaded applications that can leverage its 32 vCPUs to overcome its weak single-core performance. It achieves average throughput in static web serving, Redis SET operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks. Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage, it represents a cost-efficient option for non-critical multi-threaded processing, basic web serving, and parallelized offline tasks where peak single-core performance is not a strict requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.8xlarge is an x86_64 server instance equipped with 32 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model under the KVM hypervisor, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data reveals a significant contrast in performance: single-core metrics for CPU and compression tasks fall into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench and Passmark CPU tests, reach average tiers. Memory bandwidth remains average, though overall memory performance scores are weak. This hardware profile makes the instance suitable for parallelized workloads, static web serving, and Redis operations that benefit from multi-threaded execution but do not require high single-core speed or dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.8xlarge is a 32-vCPU, 64.0 GB RAM x86_64 server powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 CPU. It features shared CPU allocation, 5 Gbps network bandwidth, and average multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.8xlarge is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 64.0 GB RAM, and a shared Intel Xeon 8163 CPU, offering average multi-core performance.
tagline: A shared-CPU x86_64 instance offering thirty-two vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.

Shared CPU allocation model utilizing the KVM hypervisor
